problem_statement: AI coding assistants operate as black boxes—developers cannot see or control what context (dependencies, security configs, test files) drives AI code suggestions, undermining their ability to verify and trust AI-generated code for production.
magnitude_evidence: experienced developers taking 19% longer to complete tasks when using AI tools compared to working without them (METR study, cited as [1])
insufficiency_evidence: In the security-compliance scenario, AI 'generates clean, working code' but 'During code review, security team flags three issues: No rate limiting applied, Audit logging missing, Password complexity validation inconsistent'—opaque automatic context assembly misses critical dependencies
notes: Evaluation is a design-oriented cognitive walkthrough; authors note absence of empirical user study as a limitation.
